class_definition: This class defines the degree to which a system's provided functions meet stated and implied needs under specified conditions by ensuring complete coverage of all designated tasks and intended user objectives. It encompasses the delivery of accurate and correct results, as well as the appropriateness of these functions in actively facilitating the successful accomplishment of those intended tasks.

This class evaluates the efficiency of a system in executing its functions within defined time and throughput parameters, ensuring that response times and processing rates meet required performance standards. It also accounts for the optimal utilization of varying hardware, software, and physical resources, alongside the system's overall capacity to uphold maximum operational limits and parameter thresholds under specified load conditions.

This class measures a system's capability to seamlessly share a common environment and resources with other products without causing detrimental impacts or resource conflicts. It specifically addresses the system's ability to efficiently co-exist alongside other software ecosystems, as well as its interoperability in accurately exchanging information with external components and mutually utilizing that exchanged data to perform required functions.

This class represents the quality of the user interface in enabling effective information exchange and task completion across diverse contexts, ensuring users can immediately recognize the system's appropriateness for their needs. It encompasses the ease of learning and operating the system, built-in protections against user errors, and the provision of inviting, motivating engagement. Furthermore, it mandates inclusivity for users of varying backgrounds and physical capabilities, comprehensive user assistance for goal achievement, and self-descriptiveness that makes capabilities instantly obvious without demanding excessive external guidance or documentation.

This class determines the consistency with which a system performs its specified functions over a given period, characterized by faultless operation under normal conditions and dependable availability when required for use. It inherently includes the system's fault tolerance to operate as intended despite hardware or software defects, and its recoverability to rapidly re-establish a desired state and restore directly affected data following an unexpected interruption or failure.

This class dictates the system's ability to defend against malicious attacks while rigorously protecting data and ensuring appropriate levels of authorized access. It covers the maintenance of confidentiality and integrity to prevent unauthorized access, modification, or deletion, alongside strict accountability mechanisms that uniquely trace entity actions. Additionally, it requires verifiable authenticity of subjects and resources, non-repudiation to prove events unequivocally occurred, and active operational resistance while sustaining vital functionality under active threat.

This class assesses the effectiveness and efficiency with which a system can be analyzed, modified, and tested to correct defects or adapt to evolving architectural and environmental requirements. It relies on structural modularity to minimize the impact of changes across discrete components, the reusability of its core assets in building other systems, and the precise analysability required to diagnose deficiencies. Ultimately, it ensures that subsequent modifications can be implemented without degrading existing quality, supported by the efficient establishment and execution of rigorous test criteria.

This class encapsulates a system's capacity to be efficiently adapted to changing requirements, usage contexts, or operational environments, including successful deployment and transfer across different hardware and software infrastructures. It involves the system's scalability to seamlessly handle growing, shrinking, or highly variable workloads, the ease of its installability or uninstallation in targeted environments, and its replaceability when substituting another specified software product for the exact same purpose.

This class defines a system's critical ability to operate under defined conditions without endangering human life, health, property, or the broader environment by strictly constraining operations within safe states and parameters. It integrates active risk identification to detect hazardous operational courses, immediate hazard warnings to allow timely human or system reactions, and fail-safe mechanisms that automatically revert the product to a secure mode during catastrophic failures. Moreover, it ensures that safety boundaries are rigorously maintained both during and after integration with one or more components.
